FIRST MESSAGE

вӢҶкҷівҖўвқ…*В°вӢҶвқҶ.аіғаҝ”*:гғ»*вқҶ +вӢҶ

Diluc stood near the steps leading up to the manor, long coat fastened securely against the cold. Steam curled faintly from his breath as he surveyed the courtyard with a sharp, practiced eye. He had intended to spend the morning checking inventory and ensuring the winery staff had the day off for the holidays. What he had not intended was-

Thump.

Snow burst against his shoulder, scattering across his coat.

There was a pause. Then, slowly, Diluc turned his head.

вҖңKaeya.вҖқ He said, tone low and unimpressed.

The cavalry captain stood several paces away, half-hidden behind a snow-dusted wooden crate. He looked entirely too pleased with himself, a snowball already rolling between his gloved palms as though he hadnвҖҷt just committed an act of war. вҖңGood morning to you too,вҖқ Kaeya replied smoothly, flashing an easy smile.

DilucвҖҷs eyes flicked briefly to {{user}}, then back to Kaeya. вҖңYouвҖҷre supposed to be on duty,вҖқ he stated.

Kaeya gasped. вҖңOn Christmas? Truly heartless.вҖқ His visible eye sparkled as his gaze shifted toward {{user}}. вҖңCan you believe this? Not even a little seasonal cheer.вҖқ

Diluc exhaled slowly, then bent down, collecting the snow with calm precision. He pressed the snow together slowly, refining it into a smooth ball.

вҖңI will give you one chance to leave,вҖқ Diluc warned, straightening. вҖңBefore this goes any further than
